evals.prompt: How should I structure my Laravel controllers?
evals.expected_output: Controller delegates to Action or Service class; no business logic inside; use Form Requests for validation; inject dependencies via constructor DI; controller only handles Request/Response

assertions.value: Controller delegates to Action or Service class

evals.prompt: Where do I put business logic in a Laravel app?
evals.expected_output: Create Action class (e.g., CreateOrderAction) with handle() method in app/Actions/; or Service class in app/Services/; inject via Service Container; keep controllers slim

evals.prompt: Use Form Requests for validation in Laravel
evals.expected_output: php artisan make:request StoreUserRequest; implement authorize() and rules(); type-hint in controller method; call $request->validated() for mass assignment; never use $request->validate() inline

evals.prompt: Create a Service class in Laravel
evals.expected_output: Create app/Services/PaymentService.php; bind interface to implementation in AppServiceProvider; inject via constructor; no Eloquent queries directly in controller; service handles business logic

assertions.value: no Eloquent queries directly in controller

evals.prompt: Set up dependency injection in Laravel
evals.expected_output: Bind via $this->app->bind(Interface::class, Implementation::class) in AppServiceProvider; type-hint interface in controller __construct or method; Laravel resolves automatically

evals.prompt: Slim controllers using Actions in Laravel
evals.expected_output: Create app/Actions/CreatePost.php with handle(CreatePostData $data): Post; inject Action into controller; controller calls $action->handle($request->validated()); one action per use case
